steps.title: Find the cause-effect link
steps.content: The first sentence says Italian remained the default language for opera scores for several centuries. The second says singers today encounter many Italian terms in eighteenth-century opera scores. The second idea follows from the first.

steps.title: Use a result transition
steps.content: Choice B would signal contrast, Choice C would signal the last item in a sequence, and Choice D would signal concession. The relationship is result: because Italian dominated opera scoring, Italian terms still appear in scores singers use. The answer is A.
